Learn how to add and validate your envir: Learn how to add and validate your environment variables in your application.
MBC CQRS serverless framework comes with: MBC CQRS serverless framework comes with built-in support for environment variables, which allows you to do the following:
MBC CQRS serverless framework has built-: MBC CQRS serverless framework has built-in support for loading environment variables from `.env*` files into `process.env.`
The environment variables listed below a: The environment variables listed below are categorized into two types:
Variables validated by the framework's b: Variables validated by the framework's base `EnvironmentVariables` class. These are essential for the framework to function.
Variables that your application may need: Variables that your application may need based on which features you use. Add validation for these in your custom `EnvValidation` class.
The \"Required\" column indicates the ge: The "Required" column indicates the general expectation for typical applications. You should add validation rules for all variables your specific application depends on.
Running environment: local, dev, stg, pr: Running environment: local, dev, stg, prod
Application name used for table prefixes: Application name used for table prefixes
Log level: debug, verbose, info, warn, e: Log level: debug, verbose, info, warn, error, fatal
Disable event source route for API Gatew: Disable event source route for API Gateway integration
Request body size limit for JSON and URL: Request body size limit for JSON and URL-encoded data
AWS access key ID for local development: AWS access key ID for local development
AWS secret access key for local developm: AWS secret access key for local development
DynamoDB endpoint URL for local developm: DynamoDB endpoint URL for local development
Maximum size in bytes for DynamoDB item: Maximum size in bytes for DynamoDB item attributes
S3 endpoint URL for local development: S3 endpoint URL for local development
S3 bucket name for storing large DynamoD: S3 bucket name for storing large DynamoDB attributes
Step Functions endpoint URL for local de: Step Functions endpoint URL for local development
Step Functions state machine ARN for com: Step Functions state machine ARN for command processing
SNS endpoint URL for local development: SNS endpoint URL for local development
Default SNS topic ARN for event notifica: Default SNS topic ARN for event notifications
SNS topic ARN for alarm notifications (e: SNS topic ARN for alarm notifications (error alerts)
Cognito endpoint URL for local developme: Cognito endpoint URL for local development
AppSync API key for local development: AppSync API key for local development
Set `NOTIFICATION_TRANSPORTS=appsync-eve: Set `NOTIFICATION_TRANSPORTS=appsync-event` (or `appsync-graphql,appsync-event` for dual-publish) to activate the AppSync Events API transport. See [AppSyncEventsService](/docs/notification-module#appsync-events-service) for details.
Comma-separated list of active transport: Comma-separated list of active transports. Built-ins: `appsync-graphql`, `appsync-event`
AppSync Events API HTTP endpoint URL: AppSync Events API HTTP endpoint URL
Channel namespace name — must match a pr: Channel namespace name — must match a pre-created namespace in your AppSync Event API
SES endpoint URL for local development: SES endpoint URL for local development
Database connection URL for Prisma ORM: Database connection URL for Prisma ORM
AppSync Events API (optional — set NOTIF: AppSync Events API (optional — set NOTIFICATION_TRANSPORTS=appsync-event to activate)
It is standard practice to throw an exce: It is standard practice to throw an exception during application startup if required environment variables haven't been provided or if they don't meet certain validation rules. The `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/core` package makes this easy to do.
